text: weigh graph node connection force by connection size (how much value is transported by it)

reflections.context: The flow force loop in useGraphSimulation.ts was not using link.share at all — a previous flowMultiplier had been removed due to jitter concerns, and the share-based weighting was removed along with it.
reflections.outcome: Introduced shareWeight = 0.5 + 0.5 * link.share as a static per-link multiplier applied to both parent and child force lines. Range [0.5, 1.0] keeps force proportional to connection weight without ever amplifying beyond the previous baseline.
reflections.effectiveness: Clean fix — one line per force direction, no slider dependency, no risk of runaway amplification.
reflections.lesson: When removing a jitter-causing multiplier, preserve the data-driven part separately. A clamped additive form like (base + factor * data) is safer than a pure multiplicative one because it has a non-zero floor.
reflections.pattern: Similar to how semantic force stiffness is bounded: multiply by a factor but always keep a minimum so isolated nodes still feel the force.

status.comment: AI said: Restored link.share weighting via shareWeight = 0.5 + 0.5 * link.share. But I don't think it's happening. very tiny connections still have a strong influence. We have to look at incoming and outgoing relationships and weight them by their absolute flow (size). Can you double check this? I reopened
